bindings.quest_action: obtain materials to copy and seal evidence documents
bindings.quest_focus: meticulous records of the commander's extortion
bindings.quest_stakes: the garrison tightening its grip until the settlement is bled dry
bindings.quest_threat: a corrupt garrison commander abusing rank to exploit the people
bindings.quest_origin: months of double taxes, forced labor, and confiscated goods

dialogueChunks.intro: Keep your voice down, {player_name}. Walls have ears in {location}, and the ones listening don't take kindly to complaints.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} has been squeezing the merchants for months. Double taxes, forced labor on the walls, confiscated goods for 'settlement defense.' Most of it ends up in the officers' quarters. {target_npc} tried to petition for relief and was thrown in a holding cell for a day as a warning. The common folk are too afraid to speak up, but I've been keeping {quest_focus}. I need {quest_item} to copy and seal these documents. If I can get proof to the regional authority {location_hint}, this commander's reign ends.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not brave. I'm just tired of watching good people get ground down by someone who thinks rank means they can take whatever they want.

playerResponses.text: Records and evidence. Smart. I'll help you get what you need.
Going over a commander's head is dangerous. But so is doing nothing.
I don't get involved in local politics. Too many ways it goes wrong.

bindings.quest_action: gather materials to rebuild an independent forge
bindings.quest_focus: a destroyed independent forge that threatened the ruling families' monopoly
bindings.quest_stakes: the common families being permanently locked out of self-sufficiency
bindings.quest_threat: founding families who control all resources and crush any competition
bindings.quest_origin: generations of outsiders being treated as second-class citizens

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, you're new here. That means you haven't been broken yet. Listen.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The founding families of {location} control everything: the forge, the granary, the trade routes. Anyone who wasn't born into their circle gets last pick of everything. Worst jobs, worst housing, worst food. My people have been here for two generations and we're still treated like outsiders. {target_npc} tried to start an independent forge and {quest_threat} sent {enemy_type} mercenaries to wreck it. I need {quest_item} to rebuild, and I need it from somewhere the founding families don't control: out {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: They want us to stay small. Stay quiet. Stay grateful for scraps. I'm done with all three.

playerResponses.text: An independent forge changes everything. I'll bring what you need to rebuild.
Taking on the founding families is a fight you might not win. But I'll help you start.
Power struggles like this get people killed. Count me out.

bindings.quest_action: establish a hidden supply cache the corrupt guards can't touch
bindings.quest_focus: a protection racket bleeding the merchants dry
bindings.quest_stakes: every shop in the settlement being crushed under illegal fees
bindings.quest_threat: settlement guards running an extortion operation against their own people
bindings.quest_origin: weekly fees enforced through fraudulent safety inspections

dialogueChunks.intro: Pour yourself something. On the house. What I'm about to tell you deserves a drink first, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat}: pay a weekly fee or your shop gets 'inspected' and half your stock vanishes for 'safety violations.' The tavern, the bakery, the herbalist: we're all being bled dry. {target_npc} confronted the guard captain last week and got a black eye for the trouble. I've been quietly gathering {quest_item} to {quest_action} {location_hint}: a stockpile the guards can't touch. If the merchants have reserves, the guards lose their leverage.
dialogueChunks.outro: I pour drinks and I listen. I've heard every threat, every excuse, every lie. It's time to stop listening and start fighting back. Quietly.

playerResponses.text: A hidden supply cache. That's clever. Where do I start gathering?
Corrupt guards and secret stockpiles. This is going to get messy.
Getting between merchants and guards sounds like a fast way to get hurt.
